report_title: Quarterly DR Failover Test Report -- Q4 2025

steps.step: Promote RDS read replica to primary

steps.notes: 12 minutes of WAL replay. 30 minutes for consistency checks across 847 tables.
Cache miss rate initially 100%. Warm-up to 85% hit rate took 28 minutes.
All objects replicated. Maximum lag observed: 4 minutes 22 seconds.
Query performance within 15% of production baseline.

steps.step: Scale Kubernetes cluster from 2 to 8 nodes
steps.notes: EC2 instance provisioning for 6 additional nodes.

steps.step: Container image pull across new nodes
steps.notes: Container images not pre-cached in DR region ECR. Total pull size: 12.4 GB across 6 nodes. Average pull time per node: 7.5 minutes. This was the primary bottleneck.
124 pods scheduled across 8 nodes. 3 pods failed initial health checks due to database connection pool exhaustion. Resolved by increasing pool size.
All 5 Tier 1 services reporting healthy after pod restart cycle.
Health checks detected primary region failure. 3 consecutive failures at 30-second intervals.

steps.step: DNS record update to DR region

steps.notes: TTL 60 seconds. Most resolvers updated within 5 minutes.

CloudFront continued routing to primary region origin due to cached DNS. Required manual cache invalidation via CloudFront API. This step was NOT in the DR runbook. CDN cache invalidation procedure to be added to runbook.
Synthetic transactions confirmed all Tier 1 services accepting live traffic. API response times within 20% of production baseline.
P99 latency 340ms vs production baseline 280ms. Within acceptable range.
All job queues processing. No stuck jobs.

rto_assessment.notes: RTO exceeded by 92 minutes. Primary contributors: container image pull (45 min unplanned), CDN cache invalidation (22 min unplanned). Without these two issues, estimated time would have been 265 minutes -- still above RTO but closer.

data_integrity.notes: RPO met. Maximum observed lag was 4 minutes 22 seconds for S3 objects. Database WAL replay was current to within 30 seconds of failover initiation.

issues_and_remediation.issue: Container images not pre-cached in DR region ECR
issues_and_remediation.remediation: Implement ECR cross-region replication for all production images. ETA: December 15, 2025.

issues_and_remediation.issue: CDN cache invalidation not in DR runbook
issues_and_remediation.remediation: Add CloudFront cache invalidation step to DR runbook. Consider automated invalidation via Lambda trigger. ETA: December 1, 2025.

issues_and_remediation.issue: Warm standby cluster capacity insufficient (2 nodes vs 8 production)
issues_and_remediation.remediation: Increase warm standby to 4 nodes to reduce scaling time. Cost impact: approximately $2,400/month. Pending budget approval.

issues_and_remediation.issue: Database connection pool exhaustion during pod startup
issues_and_remediation.remediation: Increase default connection pool size in DR configuration. Add connection pool pre-warming to startup sequence.

sign_off.comments: DR failover test completed. RTO not met (332 min vs 240 min target). RPO met. All Tier 1 services successfully failed over and validated. Key bottlenecks identified with specific remediation actions. This test was valuable in identifying gaps that would impact a real disaster scenario. Recommend expediting remediation actions and re-testing in Q1 2026.
